page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a 393 GB drive with Premium Storage capability, while no dedicated GPUs are present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core execution. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core score, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, multi-threaded workloads leverage the 48 vCPUs to achieve average tier performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory subsystem performance is a notable highlight, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, though raw uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain in the middle tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D48s_v3 offers a high resource density of memory and vCPUs on legacy hardware, making it suitable for workloads that require substantial memory capacity but do not demand cutting-edge single-thread compute speeds. The instance is suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, all of which score in the average tier. However, it is less suited for large-scale LLM inference, where its performance on 70B models is weak, or for tasks heavily reliant on single-threaded execution.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, a 393 GB local storage volume with Premium Storage capability, and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark data reveals a performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core benchmarks, software compilation, and database operations perform in the average tier. Notably, its PassMark memory score ranks in the top 25%. This hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from high memory capacity but do not require high single-thread performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 393 GB of local storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for database and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v3 is an Intel-based virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and 393 GB of local storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine offering high memory capacity and multi-core processing for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Features 48 vCPUs and 192 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor
Includes 393 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Delivers strong memory performance, ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Exhibits average multi-core performance but weaker single-core execution
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
